title: Key custodian SoD — same identity creates AND approves a key
exclusions: Does NOT audit key usage (covered by 22.41.3); targets SoD on creation/approval.
evidence: Saved search 'kms_key_sod' (every 30 min) emits key lifecycle events where creator == approver.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 3.6.1 key-management role separation is directly enforced.
SC-12 key establishment and management requires separation of duties.
Common SoD ITGC deficiency around encryption-key management.

controlTest.positiveScenario: User alice creates a key and then approves its activation in the same audit trail; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: alice creates and bob approves; no alert.

dataSources: KMS audit logs (aws:kms, azure:keyvault, gcp:kms), HSM audit logs.
spl:
index=kms earliest=-30m (action=CreateKey OR action=ScheduleKeyActivation)
| stats values(user) as users values(action) as actions by key_id
| eval distinct_users=mvcount(mvdedup(users))
| where distinct_users<2 AND mvcount(actions)>=2
| table key_id users actions
description: Alerts when the same identity both creates a key and schedules its activation. SoD in key management is a PCI and SOX-ITGC hard requirement.
value: Provides daily evidence of key-custodian SoD rather than the common annual sample.
implementation: (1) Ingest KMS audit logs; (2) run every 30 min; (3) escalate to the crypto custodian.
visualization: Table of SoD violations, bar chart of violations by key type, single-value 'SoD-compliant key operations (30d)'.
knownFalsePositives: Automated key provisioning pipelines that run under a single service account may trigger this UC; they must be structured as two-stage with distinct approver accounts.
